name: Goguryeo
note: One of the Three Kingdoms of Korea; at its peak controlled northern Manchuria and the Korean peninsula north of the Han River. Repelled three massive Sui Chinese invasions (612–614) — the failure of those campaigns contributed to the Sui Dynasty's collapse. Finally destroyed by a Tang–Silla alliance in 668. Goguryeo identity is claimed by both modern Korea and China (as part of Gaogouli history).

figures.name: Gwanggaeto the Great
figures.significance: Expanded Goguryeo to its greatest extent — 64 walled cities and 1,400 villages conquered; his stele is the primary source for early Korean history; called 'Great King who Broadly Extended Territory'

figures.name: Eulji Mundeok
figures.significance: Defeated Sui Emperor Yang's million-man invasion (612) using strategic retreat and the Battle of Salsu; one of the greatest defensive victories in East Asian history; the Sui Dynasty never recovered
